name: chat-short-cpu
description: Scenario B / I3 CPU adaptation of the canonical chat-short v1.0.0 (inferbench@69a5abc workloads/chat-short.json): every distribution, the prefix/cancel/slow-client settings, the 600 s duration, and the seed are IDENTICAL to the canonical file; ONLY arrival_process.rate_rps is changed 8 -> 0.08. Recorded reasoning (measured 2026-07-11, evidence/i3/logs/rate-calibration.log): qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct Q4_K_M on this 4-core CPU host under llama-server 8f114a9 (-np 2 -c 8192 -t 4) sustains ~89-105 prefill tok/s and ~22.7 decode tok/s single-stream (13.8 tok/s per stream with 2 concurrent), giving a per-request service time of roughly 2 s prefill + up to ~10 s decode; at 2 slots the path saturates near ~0.17 req/s, so the canonical 8 rps would overload it ~50x. 0.08 rps targets ~0.5 utilization so open-loop queueing is present but bounded (~48 requests in 600 s). Not comparable to canonical chat-short results (different workload name/rate); the single declared variable vs chat-short-cpu runs is the topology (engine-direct vs via-gateway).
